source: CNBC — SpaceX IPO drawdown, AI infrastructure, open source models, frontier AI
summary: NVIDIA's Motron open-source model is being throttled but could rapidly reach frontier quality, making NVIDIA a potential open-source AI leader
summary_extended: Baker argues that NVIDIA has developed a strong open-source AI model called Motron that is currently being deliberately throttled — meaning NVIDIA is intentionally limiting its capabilities or release — to avoid upsetting its existing customers who compete in the AI model space. Baker believes that whenever NVIDIA chooses, it could rapidly bring Motron to near-frontier quality, positioning NVIDIA as a significant player in open-source AI in addition to its dominant hardware role. The investment implication is that NVIDIA holds a latent, underappreciated optionality in the AI software/model layer that is not yet reflected in how the market thinks about the company. Baker disclosed he holds a position in NVIDIA, and while his view is speculative ('I think,' 'I believe they could'), it is grounded in his direct knowledge of the model's current capabilities.
detail: Baker believes NVIDIA has a strong open-source model (Motron) that they are deliberately throttling to avoid upsetting customers. NVIDIA could quickly bring an American-sourced open-source model close to the frontier whenever they choose. Baker holds a position in NVIDIA.
confidence_evidence: Baker says 'I think' and 'I believe they could' — speculative but grounded in his NVIDIA position and knowledge of the model
quote_fragment: NVIDIA their new Motron model is actually really good... throttling it to a degree

ticker_audit.reasoning: The thesis is specifically about NVIDIA's open-source AI model Motron and NVIDIA's strategic decision to throttle it — NVDA is the direct subject of this thesis.
